31.reason: [Error: This line incorrectly uses scanf to read input instead of printing the popped element. The pop operation should output the top element, not read new data into the stack.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This line incorrectly uses scanf to read input instead of printing the popped element. The pop operation should output the top element, not read new data into the stack.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This line incorrectly uses scanf to read input instead of printing the popped element. The pop operation should output the value at the top of the stack, not read new input.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Should print the popped element, not scan input. Use printf("%d ", s.data[--s.top]); instead.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Should output the popped element using printf, not read input with scanf. This causes no output for pop operations and incorrectly modifies the stack.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The pop operation should output the popped element, but the code incorrectly uses scanf to read input instead of printf to output the value.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  Logical error: The pop operation should output the popped element to stdout, not read from input. It should be printf("%d ", s.data[--s.top]);
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrectly using scanf to read into the stack during a pop operation; should print the top element instead of reading input]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [In a pop operation, the program should output the top element and decrement the stack pointer, but instead it incorrectly tries to read an integer into the stack using scanf. This leads to undefined behavior and wrong output.]
